destinations.evidence: The port keeps direct parent-state scheduled-obligation scans as the single scheduler source of truth. Payload building now propagates compute_obligations and predicate-filtering SchedulerError values as PayloadBuilderError instead of logging and continuing with Vec::new().

equivalence_argument: Mapped in the port after selecting Option B: the v2.2 port does not restore SynchronousScheduler memory, but it preserves the invariant's safety property by making direct Scheduler parent-state obligation discovery fail closed instead of proceeding with empty scheduled state on read/filter failures.

related_but_insufficient_code: The old audit expected the downstream SynchronousScheduler startup-hydration implementation shape. The selected v2.2 architecture keeps direct parent-state scans and now makes payload scheduled-obligation discovery fail closed on scan/filter errors.

notes: Resolved via Option B: direct parent-state scanner is the durable v2.2 path, and Scheduler scan/filter failures are no longer converted to an empty obligation set.
rationale: The packet describes a high-confidence, load-bearing addition in the current internal reth scheduler path: startup is serialized through try_begin_startup_hydration, hydrate_from_storage must succeed, and failure aborts canonical processing rather than allowing empty in-memory state. For the v2.2 port, the old singleton is not the durable architecture; the current load-bearing behavior is the fail-closed guarantee that Scheduler state read/filter failures cannot be silently converted into no scheduled obligations.
statement: Scheduler obligation discovery must fail closed before scheduled work is treated as absent. Downstream implements this through SynchronousScheduler startup hydration from Scheduler contract storage before canonical processing; the v2.2 port preserves the same safety property through direct parent-state scans that return an error instead of continuing with an empty obligation set when Scheduler state reads or predicate filtering fail.
suggested_falsification_tests: Start with scheduler last_synced_block at zero and a state provider that fails or returns malformed Scheduler storage; assert downstream canonical processing returns StartupHydrationFailed and does not submit scheduled transactions.

In the v2.2 direct-scan port, inject a Scheduler parent-state storage failure during payload scheduled-obligation collection and assert payload building returns an error instead of treating obligations as empty.
Start with valid Scheduler storage at the canonical block hash or parent state; assert scheduled transactions are derived from that state rather than from an empty/default in-memory scheduler state.
summary: Mapped through v2.2 direct scanner fail-closed behavior.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Force Scheduler parent-state reads to fail during scheduled-obligation payload collection; expected behavior is a payload-builder error rather than an empty obligation set.
title: Canonical scheduler processing hydrates state before startup work
